mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL > 3.5%, or rolling 3-year IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and gains in payrolls (PAYEMS) signal tight labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of periods where both UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 100k, or pairwise IC < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining consumer finance availability (RSAFS) signals a demand-side imbalance, creating negative S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns exceed 5% in 40%+ of instances where HOUST > 1.2k and RSAFS < 700k, or regime-specific IC < 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 100 signal manufacturing contraction, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations adjust to lower corporate cash flows.
failure_condition: 20d SPX returns are positive in 60%+ of INDPRO < 100 periods, or rolling 3-year IC > 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% indicates strong economic momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: 60d SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of GDPC1 > 3% periods, or IC < 0.05 over 5-year horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) create real rate uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reassess risk premiums.
failure_condition: 20d SPX returns exceed 2% in 50%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL > 3% and FEDFUNDS > 4%, or interaction IC < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in unemployment (UNRATE) with stagnant payrolls (PAYEMS) signal labor market overheating, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as inflation risks dominate sentiment.
failure_condition: 5d SPX returns are positive in 50%+ of periods with UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS < 50k, or pairwise IC > 0.15.
